summary: Large PR migrating `.data` sections under `ft/chara` into C. The available dump has no per-file diff because GitHub rejected the diff as over 20,000 lines, but the PR body reports data-section source coverage increasing from 71,824 to 126,904 bytes, or 5.871000% to 10.373322%, and project progress increasing from 2 to 5 completed Event Matches while trophies stayed at 41/293.
what_changed: Migrated `.data` sections associated with `ft/chara` from assembly/data blobs into C.
Raised reported Data sections in src from 71,824 / 1,223,369 bytes to 126,904 / 1,223,369 bytes.
Raised reported Data sections source percentage from 5.871000% to 10.373322%.
Advanced the repository progress metric from 2 to 5 completed Event Matches, with trophies unchanged at 41 of 293.
The PR was very large: PR metadata reports 273 commits, 151 changed files, 7,059 additions, and 13,842 deletions, but the local PR slice does not expose changed file names or diff hunks.
smart_moves: Scoped a large migration by subsystem/directory (`ft/chara`) rather than mixing unrelated data sections across the whole project.
Used objective project metrics in the PR body to summarize matching/progress impact despite the diff being too large to display normally.
Converted a substantial amount of data-section content to C in one coordinated pass, which likely reduced assembly/data-file debt for many fighter character files at once.
decomp_lessons: Directory-scoped `.data` migrations can produce visible progress gains and are searchable/reviewable by subsystem even when individual symbols are numerous.
For very large data migrations, include before/after progress counters in the PR body so future reviewers can understand the impact when diffs are truncated or unavailable.
When a PR exceeds GitHub/API diff limits, future archival should capture changed file lists and local git diff summaries separately; otherwise postmortem evidence is limited.
naming_conventions: Evidence only supports the subsystem path convention `ft/chara`; no specific symbol, struct, or variable naming decisions are visible in the provided slice.
The PR title uses backticked path/section naming: `ft/chara` and `.data`, which is useful for searchable migration PR titles.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Primary tactic was migrating `.data` sections to C, replacing non-C data representation with C definitions.
Progress was validated at least at the aggregate level through Data sections byte/percentage counters and Event Match completion counters.
No exact alignment, padding, array typing, struct-layout, or symbol-ordering tactic is visible because the diff was unavailable in the slice.
review_feedback: Approved by r-burns on 2023-01-25 with no review body.
No issue comments or review comments were present in the slice, so there is no visible detailed reviewer guidance.
follow_up_queries: Fetch PR #717 through a local git compare or patch artifact to recover the 151 changed file names.
Inspect `ft/chara` C files around merge commit 184f1610c91039248f1b33986918255982afb619 for concrete `.data` symbol naming and array/struct patterns.
Compare with PR #718, which the corpus indicates continued the broader `.data` migration after #717.
